question: When an optimizer-facing contract (noalias, bounds, overflow-freedom, purity) turns out to be false at runtime, what should happen?

positionAssessments.position: Position 1: UB on violation, confined to explicitly marked unsafe boundaries (poison-preferring)
positionAssessments.strengths: The confinement architecture survives the critique largely intact: safe-layer-proves / unsafe-asserts is the best-attested split in the corpus (F001 safe Rust exclusivity, F006 SPARK, with F005 Fortran as the unconfined cautionary contrast), and the poison-over-immediate-UB refinement is genuinely grounded (N001 lattice; N003 shows immediate UB blocks speculation) even though every N-card carries low design-generalization confidence. If LLVM is the substrate, its contract channels (F003 noalias, N002 nsw/nuw, N004 range, N005 inbounds) map onto this position with no impedance mismatch, and the advocate's concessions were unusually honest (unquantified payoff, misleading-comparisons #5, unfinished Rust unsafe model).
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Three attacks land hard. (1) The 'forced by logic' trichotomy is refuted by the substrate itself: C005 shows LLVM gives racing non-atomic loads undef — a defined-but-nondeterministic violation semantics requiring no runtime check — and N001's freeze(poison) is a defined middle point; UB maximizes transform freedom (N001 lattice) but is not the unique check-free option, and the freedom delta is unquantified. (2) 'Safe code has zero UB from false contracts' quantifies only over single-threaded executions: data races can falsify compiler-proven noalias facts (threat model 'partially'; C002 shows race semantics is an open prior choice; C004 shows the fix is an additional type discipline the position never commits to). (3) The claimed differential tooling advantage from N006 is void — all three positions emit the same IR channels and inherit the same Alive2 ecosystem, which validates IR transforms, not frontend truth. Additionally, A005 shows the 'unremovable runtime checks' alternative is actually a precedented payoff-recovery mechanism (runtime alias checks + loop versioning), and the position has no story for how a silently-poisoned release binary is ever diagnosed. The F005 'one mistake from UB' framing and the F009 'discharges the F003 obligation' claim were both uncarded extrapolations.

positionAssessments.position: Position 2: Static proof required; unprovable contracts are never emitted; false-contract state made unrepresentable
positionAssessments.strengths: Best alignment with the ground rule that soundness may not be sacrificed: it is the only position under which the false-contract state is unrepresentable for covered code, and its fallback (omit the contract) is always sound. Its existence proofs are real and workflow-confirmed (F001 safe Rust &mut exclusivity; F006 SPARK prover-enforced anti-aliasing, ownership, side-effect-free expressions), and it is correct that strong contract emission must be gated on established source rules (F004 negative scope). Its use of N001 to argue that a false contract has unbounded blast radius by construction of the refinement lattice is the debate's most solid semantic point and survives the critique.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Its structural indictment of the checking alternative is refuted by corpus evidence it did not read: A005 documents runtime pointer checks plus multi-versioning as LLVM vectorizers' standard legality strategy, and a passed guard legitimizes emitting the same contract on dominated paths — so 'checking degenerates into don't-emit-the-contract' is false as stated, and the true cost (check execution + motion barriers, N003) is unquantified. F007 was stretched past scope (HotSpot escape analysis is profile-driven re-derivation, not checking of declared contracts; generalization confidence low). The position conflates two static regimes with opposite failure modes (by-construction typing vs prover-discharged obligations) without choosing. Its 'exactly three coherent policies' case analysis omitted speculate-and-deoptimize (J001/J002 — sound, no source UB, and per J002 enables optimizations static compilers cannot do; plausibly rejectable for AOT on J005 warmup/memory grounds, but it had to be enumerated). And it shares Position 1's concurrency hole: proofs quantify over race-free executions, so 'unrepresentable' fails one layer down until the memory model is decided (C002, C004). Proof burden, rejection rates, and escape-hatch pressure are all unquantified and historically adoption-limiting (F006 negative scope).

positionAssessments.position: Position 3: Graduated obligations — debug-checked, trapped/conservative when cheap, assumed only in explicitly marked release contexts, per-fact and auditable
positionAssessments.strengths: Closest to the post-critique convergence point: it already contains Position 2's top tier (compiler-proven facts assumable in every build at zero runtime cost — the corpus's best-attested wins, F001/F005/F006, are all in this tier) and Position 1's marked-assumption contexts, and it is the only position with any violation-observability story (debug checks; Go's race-detection halts in C002 are now carded precedent for trap-with-report). The substrate genuinely supports graduation: every carded contract channel has an assume form and a sound omission fallback (N002 unflagged/flagged, N004, N005 inbounds/non-inbounds), freeze is a documented containment point (N001), and — decisively, supplied by the critic's own evidence — A005 gives the middle tier a carded mechanism for aliasing facts in loop-shaped code (runtime pointer checks + versioning buying assume-grade inner loops). It is also the only position structurally able to accommodate per-contract-class decomposition, which the purity evidence gap suggests is necessary.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Its distinctive middle tier is uncarded exactly where the payoff is highest: general scoped-noalias disjointness is a whole-execution property with no carded runtime check (A005 covers only bounded pointer-range checks in loops), and purity has no dynamic-check evidence at all — so on those contract classes it currently degenerates to Position 1 plus mode-matrix complexity, as the critic charged. The checked tier's lowering mechanics (trap intrinsics, soundness of combined check+flag emission, dominance ordering) have no card, so 'all three are native lowerings, no new semantics' is unsupported at the position's differentiating tier — and that tier is where F003-negative-scope-style frontend soundness bugs live. N003 was read in reverse (guards pessimize; that is the cost, not evidence of compatibility). Debug/release divergence, marking metastasis under performance pressure, and cross-mode semantic equivalence are all real, unsized risks. The Ada/Zig branding has zero corpus support (caveats.md confirms no Zig coverage).

keyTradeoffs: Assumption strength vs blast radius: UB-backed assertion (F003, N002, N004, N005) grants maximal transform freedom by the N001 lattice, but a single false contract licenses unbounded, silent, non-local miscompilation; defined violation semantics (undef for racing loads C005, bounded racy outcomes C002) shrink blast radius without runtime checks but invalidate some rewrites (N001: per-use nondeterminism breaks x*2 -> x+x). The size of the freedom delta is unquantified anywhere in the corpus.

Static proof coverage vs expressiveness and burden: proof-gated emission makes false contracts unrepresentable (F001, F006) but rejects or deoptimizes exactly the dynamic-aliasing, FFI, and concurrency-adjacent code where the threat model says verification fails ('sometimes no', 'often no', 'no') and where payoff may be highest; annotation burden, prover time, and rejection rates are unquantified and F006's negative scope flags usability costs.

Runtime checking cost vs recovered assumption plus observability: checks pin code motion (N003) and cost cycles/code size (A005 versioning trades code size for assume-grade loops), but a passed check legitimizes the contract on dominated paths and checking is the only tier with any post-violation diagnosis story (C002 Go race halts as precedent). Check overhead after modern elision is unmeasured in this corpus.

Uniform policy vs per-contract-class decomposition: noalias-class disjointness (whole-execution property, runtime-checkable only in loop-bounded form per A005), bounds/overflow (per-operation checkable, N002/N004/N005), and purity (static-only evidence, F006; no IR violation-semantics card) have different checkability, provability, and IR channels — one violation-semantics policy for all four may be a category error only Position 3 can even express.

Memory-model choice caps every position: UB races (C/C++/Rust/Swift per C002) make racy contract falsification moot by fiat but import maximal blast radius into 'safe' code; bounded races (Java/JS/Go) preserve diagnosability but forbid some F002-class optimizations on possibly-shared memory (C003) unless C004-style type-level non-sharing is adopted — which couples this debate to the type-system design.

Debug/release graduation vs shipped-binary fidelity: checked builds give the only carded diagnosability, but you then test semantics you do not ship; divergence-escape rates are completely unevidenced.
evidenceGaps: The A/D/C/J card series (phase2-arrays/dispatch/concurrency/jit findings) exists but carries no adversarial verification votes — provisional per ground rule 3 — and was absent from the debate's assigned reading; the debate brief's card ranges and file list are inconsistent (process defect, confirmed by inspection: files dated Jul 1 postdate caveats.md's Jun 30 'no concurrency coverage' statement).

No quantified performance data for any tier: caveats.md and F002's negative scope state documentation evidence does not quantify magnitude; every comparative performance claim by every advocate is structural conjecture. A three-way benchmark (UB-asserted vs runtime-checked+versioned vs proof-gated) on aliasing/bounds/overflow kernels is the single most decision-capable missing artifact.

Race semantics is an open prior decision: C002's negative scope says the UB-vs-bounded choice 'is an open debate question' with no performance quantification; every 'proven facts stay true at runtime' claim in all three positions depends on it, and C004-style type-level non-sharing is the only carded route to closing it.

Checked-tier mechanics uncarded: no card covers trap intrinsic costs, soundness of combined check+flag emission (dominance ordering), or whether llvm.assume after a passed check recovers assumption-grade optimization.

Debug-mode dynamic checkability uncarded: no evidence on Miri/UBSan/TSan-class detection of false noalias/inbounds/nsw/purity assertions or its cost — this decides whether Positions 1 and 3 have a debuggability story at all.
Purity has no violation-semantics evidence: the only purity card (F006) is restriction-based and static; no IR channel or dynamic check for purity violations is carded.

Violation observability post-deployment: no card addresses how a false contract in a poison-silent release binary is ever discovered (canary builds, trap-with-report); C002's Go race-halt is the only adjacent precedent.
Contract survival through lowering: F003 and F009 negative scopes both condition soundness/value on metadata being true and surviving lowering; no end-to-end measurement exists.

Linkage-boundary falsification: D008 (ELF semantic interposition) and D004 (codegen-unit/LTO boundaries) show contracts can be falsified by linking, not just by programmers; no position addressed emission policy for exported symbols, and the threat model has no row for it.
Substrate undecided: LLVM channels vs MLIR MemRef distinct_objects/in-bounds (A001) offer different violation-semantics vocabularies; both Positions 1 and 2 argued from LLVM specifics as if decided.

Unsafe/marked-region proliferation: no empirical evidence on whether explicit-marking or unsafe-block discipline holds under performance pressure, or on defect density inside audited assertion regions.
leadingCandidate: Position 3 (graduated obligations), amended post-critique: compiler-proven contracts assumable in every build mode; human-asserted contracts only in per-fact explicitly marked contexts, dynamically checkable in debug builds; A005-style runtime-check-plus-code-versioning as the carded middle mechanism where checks exist; conservative no-contract fallback at unverifiable boundaries (FFI, int-ptr casts, per the threat model); purity and general scoped-noalias restricted to proven-or-conservative until dynamic-check evidence exists. This subsumes Position 2's top tier and Position 1's confinement while being the only position with an observability story.
rationale: The outcome is research_needed rather than a design lean for three reasons. First, the debate demonstrably ran on a stale evidence view: I verified that the C/J/A/D card files exist (notes/phase2-{concurrency,jit,arrays,dispatch}-findings.jsonl, dated after caveats.md), all three advocates wrongly declared them absent, and those cards reverse load-bearing shared premises — C005 refutes Position 1's 'any defined semantics forfeits assumption' trichotomy, A005 refutes Position 2's 'checking forfeits the payoff' structure, and J001/J002 expose a fourth policy Position 2's exhaustive case analysis omitted. Per the corpus's own standing rules (decision-gates promotion requirements; caveats.md 'no final design decisions from this plan'), a debate whose central arguments were falsified by unadmitted cards must be re-run, and those cards must first be adversarially verified since they currently carry no votes. Second, the critic's sharpest point is confirmed by the cards: whether 'contract false at runtime' is even a complete question depends on the race-semantics choice, which C002 explicitly marks open — though I weigh this as a dependency to be decided first or jointly, not full incommensurability, because the single-threaded core of the question (what a false human assertion means) is well-posed regardless and the positions converge substantially on it: all three agree proven facts are assumable everywhere, unproven assertion is never ambient, and unverifiable boundaries get conservative fallback. Third, the residual disagreement — the asserted-but-unproven middle tier — sits exactly where the corpus is empty: zero performance quantification (caveats.md), no checked-lowering or trap-cost cards, no dynamic-checker cards, no purity violation-semantics card, no observability evidence. That is 'evidence too thin', not 'mechanism plausible but untested', so research_needed dominates prototype_needed; the prototype gate comes after the missing cards exist. Position 3 (amended) is named leading because its weaknesses after critique are evidence gaps, while Position 1's central theorem ('safe code has zero UB') is refuted as stated pending the memory-model decision and Position 2's central structural claim is refuted by A005 — but it is not 'clearly leading' in the design_candidate_later sense: its distinctive tier is uncarded on the highest-value contract class, and per decision-gates no position has met even the prototype_required bar.
followUps: Formally admit the A/D/C/J card series into the corpus and run adversarial verification on them (they currently lack verification votes, unlike F/N cards); fix the debate-brief inconsistency between cited card ranges and the assigned file list; then re-run this debate on the full corpus.

Run the memory-model / race-semantics debate (UB races vs bounded outcomes vs C004-style type-level non-sharing) as a decision prior to or joint with violation-semantics; C002 marks it open and every position's soundness story depends on it.

Produce the three-way benchmark caveats.md says is missing: UB-asserted vs runtime-checked(+A005-style versioning) vs proof-gated contracts on representative aliasing/bounds/overflow kernels, measuring speed, code size, and check-elision rates — this operationalizes Position 3's 'cheap' threshold and tests Position 2's forfeiture claim.

Card the checked tier's mechanics: LLVM trap/check lowering cost, soundness rules for co-emitting checks with nsw/range/inbounds flags (dominance ordering), and whether llvm.assume after a passed check recovers assumption-grade optimization.

Card debug-mode dynamic checkers (Miri, UBSan, TSan class): which asserted contracts (noalias, inbounds, nsw, purity) they can falsify and at what cost — this decides whether the checkable-in-dev/assumed-in-release story exists.
Decompose the topic per contract class and source purity-specific violation-semantics evidence (IR channel, dynamic checkability); F006's static-only evidence cannot carry a purity policy.

Decide or dual-track the substrate: map contract channels and violation semantics for MLIR MemRef (distinct_objects, in-bounds; A001-A004) against LLVM's, since both UB-advocating positions argued from LLVM specifics as if the backend were decided.

Design and card the post-deployment observability story: trap-with-report semantics (Go race-halt precedent, C002), check-enabled canary builds, and how a poison-silent release miscompile is ever attributed to a false contract.
Add a linkage-boundary row to the threat model and card emission policy for exported symbols under ELF interposition (D008) and codegen-unit/LTO boundaries (D004).

Per decision-gates, before any position can pass prototype_required: a lowering sketch for the marked-assume path plus an Alive2-style validation plan (N006) demonstrating asserted source contracts survive lowering and measurably fire F002-class optimizations.

question: How should the language express non-interference (aliasing, mutation exclusivity) — the strongest optimizer enabler in the seed corpus? (Note: the 'strongest enabler' framing is itself corpus-flagged as selection-biased per misleading-comparisons entry 1 and caveats.md; the defensible framing is 'well-attested in selected sources.')

positionAssessments.position: P1: Ownership/borrowing baked into the type system; exclusive &mut-style references as the safe default
positionAssessments.strengths: Post-attack, this position retains the corpus's only adversarially-verified exemplars of machine-checked non-interference at per-reference granularity (F001: safe Rust makes overlapping compute(&x, &mut x) impossible) and the only attested static route to data-race freedom (C004: Send/Sync + exclusivity), which matters under the non-negotiable soundness rule. It has the only concrete pipeline architecture (F009: exploit ownership in a high-level IR before conservative lowering). Most decisively, the critic's own convergence analysis strengthens it: P2's sound (checked) variant rests on ownership for access types per F006, and P3 concedes ownership fallback wherever disjointness verification is hard — ownership machinery is the common denominator of every sound variant surveyed. Its advocate's concessions were also the most honest and complete.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Four attacks landed. (1) 'Only compiler-verified mechanism' is falsified by F001/F006 — SPARK's boundary anti-aliasing is also machine-checked, so P1's uniqueness claim was spin. (2) The 'design attractor / independent convergence' argument has no card on SPARK/Rust lineage. (3) F004 was overclaimed: 'naturally proves fresh-return' contradicts F004's negative scope (lifetime/provenance/escape rules required first) and memory-object-model.md's open questions. (4) The 'double dividend' is non-additive: the interior-mutability route to safe shared-state concurrency is the same feature misleading-comparisons #2 names as puncturing shared-reference noalias facts (plausible, uncarded — needs its own card). Residual structural weaknesses: coverage-frontier size (unsafe/UnsafeCell/raw pointers/callbacks/FFI) is unmeasured; Rust's aliasing model is unfinalized (caveats.md); 'UB-grade freedom on safe code' ignored C005's undef-not-UB racing-load semantics; zero quantified payoff; and exclusivity-by-default-everywhere is a one-way door priced by no benchmark.

positionAssessments.position: P2: Opt-in/default function-boundary contracts (Fortran dummy-argument / checked-restrict direction)
positionAssessments.strengths: Correctly locates where language design adds value over intraprocedural analysis: opaque call boundaries (F002 names the analyses and the hazard). F005 is genuine evidence that a shipped performance language runs a default-no-alias-at-boundaries regime the compiler may assume, with aliasing as the marked case (TARGET). Signature contracts lower directly onto the attribute half of LLVM's vocabulary (F003 noalias attributes, F004 return noalias). The checked variant has a real exemplar (F006 SPARK anti-aliasing), and the layering precedent (SPARK atop Ada) gives the reversibility argument some grounding.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: The critic's refutation of the load-bearing premise is verified: F003's positive scope attaches alias.scope/noalias metadata to individual loads, stores, calls, and fences, so 'the optimizer consumes facts at exactly one granularity: the signature' is false and the 'lossless one-to-one lowering' argument collapses to half of LLVM's vocabulary. The position then equivocates fatally: the headline Fortran-grade assumed-conformance form is inadmissible under the soundness ground rule (F001 negative scope: depends on programmer truthfulness; F003: violation is UB, silently, with no diagnostic), while the admissible checked form imports ownership machinery at every heap-pointer case (F006: SPARK checks boundary contracts only atop access-type ownership and banned side effects) — forfeiting the simplicity and reversibility selling points, which belong exclusively to the unsound form. 'Decades of production evidence' mischaracterizes F005 (specification of what compilers MAY assume, not empirical performance data). The trust-boundary symmetry move launders an asymmetry: every ordinary signature becomes a UB-bearing trust point vs localized greppable unsafe blocks. No story for higher-order functions, callbacks, or globals.

positionAssessments.position: P3: First-class verified alias-set/effect annotations over named regions
positionAssessments.strengths: Only position that natively addresses the finer-grained half of LLVM's vocabulary (F003's named scopes/domains and per-access sets) and can directly declare the strongest known contract, 'returns fresh region' (F004). Its verified-annotation discipline is exactly the emission rule memory-object-model.md mandates. It was also the only position to flag the loop/element-level granularity ceiling — which the critic correctly elevated to a debate-wide blind spot — and the only one to gesture at hybrid complementarity with ownership.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Under the corpus's standing rule (design proposals must cite exact evidence cards) it is not yet a citable design: zero exemplar cards exist for any first-class region/effect language (caveats.md lists Halide, ISPC, Chapel, Mojo, MLIR as uncovered; no DPJ/Cyclone/Koka cards). F006 is stretched from restriction-checking (banning hard cases) to disjointness-proof (discharging them) — SPARK's decidability is bought by the bans. The 'near-identity lossless lowering' claim is uncarded and internally undercut: classifying every access into a region and proving membership is exactly the lossy projection step it accuses rivals of. The 'generated/ugly-by-design' premise is not in the charter (ergonomics-may-be-sacrificed does not entail machine generation), and the verifier — the position's entire soundness mechanism — has no feasibility, cost, or discharge-rate evidence. The separate-compilation attack on P1 was a strawman (ownership facts are signature-carried types). It concedes collapse into an ownership sub-discipline at the pointer-rich frontier.

keyTradeoffs: Trust-boundary placement, not existence: all sound variants converge on checked discharge; what differs is where the unchecked residue lives — localized opt-in unsafe blocks (P1), every function signature in assumed mode (P2), or verifier holes at FFI/callback boundaries (P3) — and the corpus measures none of these surfaces.

Fact coverage vs expressiveness cliff: exclusive-by-default references maximize the program surface carrying static facts (F001/F002 payoff classes) but push pointer-rich structures (graphs, intrusive lists, callback-heavy code) past an unsafe frontier of unmeasured size; boundary/region contracts keep the base type system simpler but deliver facts only where annotated/checkable.

Lowering fidelity vs granularity: signature contracts map 1:1 onto LLVM's attribute half but cannot express instruction-level alias.scope metadata (F003); type-derived facts cover more surface but project lossily (misleading-comparisons #2, #5); region annotations claim the metadata half but interpose an unevidenced verifier.

Sequential vs concurrency dividends trade rather than add: the interior-mutability mechanism that enables safe shared-state concurrency (C004's ecosystem) punctures the shared-references-are-immutable noalias fact — the same design cannot maximize both without a carded resolution.

Irreversibility under unquantified benefit: pervasive exclusivity reshapes every API and datatype; default-no-alias-at-boundaries becomes semantic bedrock for every compiled program; both one-way doors are currently priced by zero benchmarks (F002 negative scope; caveats.md).

Boundary/object granularity vs loop/element granularity: all three positions state whole-object or per-signature facts, but F002's payoff list includes loop-centric transformations, and element/iteration-level disjointness — what vectorizers consume — is entirely uncovered (caveats.md: arrays, layout, Halide, ISPC, Futhark, Chapel all missing). The debate may be scoped at the wrong granularity for an optimizer-first language.
evidenceGaps: Zero quantified performance data for any non-interference mechanism (F002 negative scope; caveats.md: documentation evidence does not quantify magnitude). No noalias-on/off benchmark deltas exist for any design.

Frontier size unmeasured: what fraction of performance-critical code falls outside each discipline (unsafe/UnsafeCell/raw-pointer density in ownership languages; undischargeable contracts in SPARK-style checking). This is the decisive variable in the critic's sharpest tension and no card touches it.

memory-object-model.md prerequisites unanswered (object identity, provenance, interior-pointer stability across moves, uninitialized bytes, unsafe/callback/signal/finalizer/FFI invalidation) — these block sound noalias/lifetime emission for every position equally.

LLVM pipeline reliability unmeasured: F003's negative scope conditions soundness on metadata surviving lowering and being consumed; no card covers preservation/exploitation of scoped noalias metadata through -O2/-O3, and the widely believed Rust noalias-toggling history is uncarded background knowledge needing verification.

Card ranges N001-N006, A001-A010, D001-D010, and J001-J005 named in the debate brief were absent from all six readable files; only F001-F009 and C001-C006 were available. If those cards exist, the debate must be re-weighed.

A fourth mechanism family is unargued and uncovered: C006 (immutability-after-construction/publication safety) was cited by no advocate, and caveats.md lists all functional/value-semantics routes (Haskell/ML/Futhark) as unverified — 'make it immutable' is the cheapest non-interference fact per F002's writes-are-the-hazard framing and was never on the table.

No cards on region/effect-system languages (DPJ, Cyclone, Koka, MLIR effect interfaces) — P3's entire mechanism family — nor on annotation error rates for restrict-like contracts, nor on contract/effect inference feasibility for internal functions.

Checker as trusted computing base: no cards on borrow-checker/contract-verifier implementation defect history or compile-time cost, so the soundness ground rule cannot currently distinguish positions on implementation soundness (vs paper soundness).
No position has a carded story for higher-order functions/callbacks (misleading-comparisons #2 names callbacks as a fact-destroyer) or for global/ambient state mod-ref at boundaries.

The critic's interior-mutability-vs-noalias tension and the source-model-to-IR race-semantics mapping (C003 negative scope, C005 undef vs UB) are plausible and load-bearing but not directly carded — both need dedicated cards before the concurrency dividend can be counted.
leadingCandidate: Position 1 — checked ownership/exclusive-mutation in the type system as the core non-interference discharge mechanism, expected to land as the core of a hybrid (signature-level mod/ref summaries as the lowering surface per F003/F004, SPARK-style checked boundary contracts, and a C006-style immutability-after-construction tier), with the exclusivity-by-default-for-every-reference commitment explicitly NOT yet settled.
rationale: P1 leads for structural reasons that survive the critic's verified attacks, not because its advocate's case stood intact. What fell: P1's uniqueness claim (SPARK's boundary anti-aliasing is also machine-checked per F001/F006), the design-attractor argument (no lineage card), the F004 'naturally proves' claim (contradicted by F004's own negative scope), and the additive 'double dividend' framing (interior mutability trades the concurrency win against the noalias win). What stands: F001 and C004 remain the corpus's only adversarially-verified exemplars of statically discharged non-interference and data-race freedom — the only mechanisms meeting the non-negotiable soundness rule with evidence rather than promise. Decisively, the critic's convergence analysis cuts FOR ownership rather than against a verdict: P2's central granularity premise was refuted by its own key card (F003 metadata is instruction-level), its admissible checked form imports ownership machinery at every heap-pointer case (F006), and P3 concedes ownership fallback at its hard frontier while owning zero exemplar cards — so every sound road runs through ownership-like discharge, making it the common denominator, not a bet among equals. This is design_candidate_later rather than research_needed because the mechanism-family question has a clear leader on current evidence; it is design_candidate_later rather than a decision because (a) no payoff magnitude or frontier-size measurement exists anywhere in the corpus, (b) memory-object-model.md's prerequisites block sound emission for all positions, (c) an entire unargued mechanism family (C006 immutability/value semantics) and P3's region family have no coverage and could still reshape the design, and (d) the N/A/D/J card series named in the brief were never available. Per the corpus's standing rule, this names a leading candidate pending verification; it does not settle surface syntax, defaults, or the hybrid composition.
followUps: Locate or confirm nonexistence of card series N001-N006, A001-A010, D001-D010, J001-J005 referenced by the debate brief; if they exist in the corpus, re-run this debate with them on the table.

Produce the single most decision-relevant artifact: controlled noalias-on/off benchmark deltas (e.g., Rust mutable-noalias toggle, Flang dummy-argument aliasing assumptions toggle) on realistic workloads, converting F002's payoff-in-kind into payoff-in-magnitude.

Measure the frontier: survey unsafe/UnsafeCell/raw-pointer density in performance-critical Rust code and SPARK contract-discharge rates, to size the 'discharged subset' each discipline actually covers — the variable the critic's sharpest tension turns on.

Treat memory-object-model.md's open questions (object identity, provenance, interior-pointer stability across moves, uninitialized bytes, FFI/callback invalidation) as a blocking prerequisite work item before any noalias/lifetime emission design, per its own emission rule.

Create adversarially-verified cards for the two uncovered mechanism families: (a) immutability/freeze/value-semantics routes (C006 direction; Haskell/ML/Futhark per caveats.md) and (b) region/effect systems (DPJ, Koka, MLIR side-effect interfaces, Halide/ISPC) — then decide whether a fourth-position debate is warranted.

Card the interior-mutability vs shared-reference-noalias tension and the historical Rust/LLVM noalias-toggling record (currently unverified background knowledge), since both bear directly on P1's coverage and on LLVM-channel reliability.

Prototype the hybrid architecture implied by the evidence: exclusivity-checked core + signature-level mod/ref summaries + self-owned high-level IR exploiting ownership facts before LLVM (F009 route), and instrument scoped-noalias metadata survival through full -O2/-O3 pipelines (F003 negative scope).

Specify the escape-hatch race semantics (C002 UB-vs-bounded decision for unsafe/FFI code) and the exact source-model-to-LLVM mapping given C005's undef-racing-load semantics — required residue under every position.

Investigate element/iteration-level disjointness expression (arrays, loop-carried facts for vectorization) — the granularity all three positions miss and caveats.md flags as uncovered; gather Halide/ISPC/Futhark/polyhedral evidence before fixing the aliasing model's granularity.

question: How much array, layout, and loop structure should the language surface to the optimizer?

positionAssessments.position: Structured-arrays-first (shapes in types, affine-restricted indexing, first-class parallel/reduction loops, marked escape for irregular code)
positionAssessments.strengths: Best convergence between the adversarially verified findings and the precedent base after the critic's attacks: the confirmed payload is non-interference around writes (F001, F002), and every genuinely checked precedent in the corpus (Rust exclusivity per F001, SPARK ownership per F006, Futhark uniqueness/sizes per A008) achieved checkability by restricting the model — which is this position's move. Its central tractability intuition is directly carded (A003: affine restriction makes dependence analysis efficient and reliable), the target stack natively carries structured facts (A001-A004), and A005's documented consumer list (alias/dependence facts, reductions, trip counts, control-flow shape) is exactly what shapes-in-types plus declared loop kinds supply by construction. It also degrades honestly: unsupported claims were about ease and losslessness, not about the direction itself.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Its decisive differentiator — 'checkable rather than promised' — is uncarded for cross-iteration independence: the cited precedents (A010 DO CONCURRENT, F005 Fortran) are promise-based per the cards' own text, and no card attests any system statically checking loop-body independence; the critic's checkability bait-and-switch charge stands. F005 is causally misattributed (the card credits Fortran's aliasing contracts, not array-ness), F009 is non-discriminating, and 'lowering structure is easy / nearly lossless frontend' is asserted against the dossier's own open question on fact consumption. The escape-hatch/views boundary where every emitted shape/noalias fact can be invalidated (A001 negative scope, F003 UB) is waved at, not designed, and the workload share of the affine fragment is a complete unknown — if low, the design optimizes the wrong center.

positionAssessments.position: Algorithm/schedule separation (pure algorithm layer, distinct schedule layer for loop order/tiling/vectorization/layout, auto-derivable with override)
positionAssessments.strengths: The only position where optimization decisions are themselves first-class program structure rather than facts to trust or re-derive; aligns with the factoring MLIR Linalg independently adopted (A002) and the staged Vector lowering path (A004). Purity genuinely delivers F002-style non-interference for the interior of the dense fragment, the ISPC turn (baking execution strategy into algorithms is hardware-fragile, A007) is a real point against explicit-control rivals, and its scope honesty (primary surface for the dense/affine fragment only, with an escape hatch) was the most candid framing in the debate.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Thinnest evidence base of the three and the critic's sharpest hits land unanswered: the sole existence proof is one provisional tutorial card (A006) for a system caveats.md explicitly lists as lacking verified coverage; 'self-discharged obligations' fails at the host-buffer boundary, where user-supplied overlapping storage makes compiler-emitted noalias facts false (F003 UB) — its structural soundness is contractual exactly where it attacked the contracts rival; the storage-layout plank has zero cards (A006 lists only loop transformations); 'lowers deterministically' is contradicted by A004's own negative scope; the auto-scheduling half is conceded evidence-free, so as evidenced the position is mandatory expert hand-scheduling; and bufferization internalizes the write-interference problem with only A008 (itself flagged as needing further evidence) as mechanism.

positionAssessments.position: General memory plus checkable contracts (pointer/slice base model; disjointness/layout/alignment/contiguity as verified, runtime-checked, or explicitly-unsafe annotations lowering to LLVM legality machinery)
positionAssessments.strengths: The only position whose central fact family (disjointness) is backed end-to-end by adversarially verified cards from language rule (F001, F005, F006) to IR carrier (F003, F004); the only one with graceful degradation outside the dense fragment (conservative code, not rejection or escape hatch); and its substrate observation is correct and important — the structured stack itself bottoms out in a contract-bearing memory layer (A001 distinct_objects), so contracts are the load-bearing layer under every rival. Its verify-or-check-or-gate posture is the right soundness discipline for this project.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Its evidence lead is explicitly flagged by the corpus's own discipline notes as selection bias (caveats.md; misleading-comparisons entry 1), not established importance. Three of its four fact families (layout, alignment, contiguity) have no carded sound, consumed encoding — the 'best-attested facts' framing overstates by most of the position's surface area. A005's runtime checks are vectorizer-internal speculation, not a user-contract validation mechanism, so the claimed soundness template is uncarded. F006 partially backfires: the corpus's only static-checking precedent achieved checkability by restricting the memory model, making 'checkable contracts over unrestricted pointers' the one configuration no card attests. It concedes no path to loop-nest transformation parity (tiling/fusion/interchange evidence sits with A002/A003), and whether an explicitly-unsafe tier satisfies the soundness red line is asserted, not argued.

keyTradeoffs: Tractability vs coverage: affine/regular restriction demonstrably buys dependence-analysis reliability and loop-nest transformability (A003, A002), but forfeits or pessimizes irregular/sparse/pointer-heavy code whose workload share is entirely unmeasured; general memory retains coverage but has no carded route to loop-nest transformation parity.

Checkability vs generality: every checked precedent in the corpus (Rust, SPARK/F006, Futhark/A008) obtained checkability by restricting the memory or update model; checkable facts over an unrestricted model is the unattested configuration — so the more general the base model, the more the soundness rule forces either unpriced runtime checks or an unsafe tier.

Structural soundness vs boundary contracts: each design relocates rather than eliminates the trust boundary (structured core's escape hatch and views, pure layer's host-supplied buffers, contracts language's unsafe tier); which boundary is cheapest to police is the actual discriminator and the corpus is empty on it.

Guaranteed facts vs guaranteed transformations: A005 and A010 jointly show no design can promise optimization outcomes, only fact delivery — an optimizer-first language must define what it guarantees (facts surfaced, preserved, consumed) versus best-effort (transformations performed), and each position prices this differently.

Exclusive winner vs layered composition: MLIR's own stack (A001 contract substrate, A002/A003 structured ops, A004 staged vectors) instantiates all three positions simultaneously; the real decision variables are the default surface, where the checking burden falls, and the failure mode of a violated fact — treating the positions as mutually exclusive may be a framing error.
evidenceGaps: End-to-end fact consumption is unvalidated for every position's lowering path: no evidence that shape/affine/parallel facts or noalias metadata emitted by a frontend survive bufferization and lowering and actually change LLVM vectorizer/parallelizer decisions (dossier open question; F003 caveat; misleading-comparisons entry 5).

Failure-mode semantics for violated facts (static rejection vs runtime check vs trap vs unsafe promise vs conservative fallback vs IR UB) — the dossier poses it, no card answers it, and it is where the three positions genuinely differ.

All A-series cards (A001-A010) are unverified direct extraction from systems caveats.md lists as lacking verified coverage, against a measured base rate of 2 refuted and 2+ dropped claims from the same pipeline; nobody consulted notes/refuted-and-unverified.jsonl.

No card attests any system statically checking cross-iteration independence of loop bodies — the checkability claim central to structured-first is unattested; equally, no card attests a mechanism for validating user-written disjointness/contiguity contracts at call boundaries or its runtime/code-size cost.

Workload census absent: what fraction of intended programs fits the regular/affine (or pure/schedulable) fragment, and what the fallback costs for the remainder; sparse/irregular/graph workloads have zero corpus coverage.

Trust-boundary semantics uncovered for all designs: how externally supplied storage (FFI, callers, I/O buffers) acquires shape/disjointness/alignment facts, and views/slices aliasing over shaped arrays (A001 negative scope).

FP reduction ordering/reassociation policy: A005 makes math flags a legality input, so numeric semantics gate all three positions' parallel/vector stories; no card addresses determinism or reassociation contracts.
Degradation under dynamic/symbolic shapes is uncovered for all positions (runtime sizes weaken trip counts, bounds inference, and static contract discharge alike).
No quantified performance, compile-time, or code-size evidence for any position (caveats.md standing constraint); all comparative claims in the debate are architectural.
The N001-N006, D001-D010, C001-C006, and J001-J005 card series named as citable were absent from the assigned read set for every participant; the debate ran on F and A cards only.
leadingCandidate: Structured-arrays-first as the default surface, explicitly reframed as the top layer of a composition: a structured/affine-checked core over a contract-bearing general-memory substrate (the F003/A001 layer), with an optional schedule layer confined to the pure fragment — not structured-first as an exclusive design.
rationale: The verified corpus establishes exactly three things: non-interference around writes is the valuable payload (F001, F002), LLVM has carriers for it whose soundness is conditional on frontend obligations and metadata survival (F003, F004), and the only precedents that made such facts checked rather than promised did so by restricting the model (F006, and A008/Rust per F001). That pattern favors a restricted structured core as the default surface, and it is why structured-arrays-first leads despite the critic landing real hits on it: its direction is carded (A003) even where its decisive claims (checkable independence, lossless lowering) are not. The contracts position's apparent evidentiary lead is discounted by the corpus's own discipline notes as selection bias, and its distinctive configuration — checkable contracts over unrestricted pointers — is the one no card attests; schedule separation rests on a single provisional tutorial card, lost its soundness-by-construction claim at the host-buffer boundary, and has an entire plank (layout scheduling) and an entire half (auto-scheduling) with zero evidence. But the outcome cannot be design_candidate_later: the critic demonstrated that every position's core differentiating mechanism is unattested, that all three relocate rather than eliminate the trust boundary, and that the debate's true decision variables (default surface, checking-burden location, violated-fact failure mode) were argued by no one. These are mechanism-plausibility questions that more documentation research cannot close — caveats.md itself says documentation evidence cannot quantify what is missing — so the discriminating next step is a spike: prove facts emitted by a structured frontend survive lowering and change optimizer decisions, prove cross-iteration independence is checkable for the affine fragment, and price the runtime checks and boundary enforcement each design needs. Not research_needed alone, because the A-card verification pass, while necessary, cannot settle consumption or checkability; not evidence_conflict, because the cards are thin and biased rather than opposed; not no_decision, because a leading direction and a concrete discriminating experiment both exist.
followUps: Build the end-to-end fact-consumption spike (dossier open question 5): a minimal frontend emitting shaped/affine/parallel facts through Linalg/Affine/Vector and, separately, noalias metadata through the direct LLVM path; measure which facts survive bufferization/lowering and whether they measurably change loop/SLP vectorizer decisions on ~5 representative kernels.

Prototype the checkability claim that discriminates position 1 from position 3: implement static cross-iteration independence checking for affine-bodied parallel loops, and measure what fraction of representative kernels type-check statically vs require runtime checks vs must fall back to promises.

Run the adversarial verification pass on A001-A010 (priority: A002, A003, A006, A008), consulting notes/refuted-and-unverified.jsonl first and applying the measured 2-refuted/25-verified base rate as the discount for anything still unverified.

Convene a dedicated decision on failure-mode taxonomy (dossier open question 2): for each fact family (disjointness, shape, alignment, contiguity, independence), fix static-reject / runtime-check / trap / unsafe-promise / conservative-fallback semantics, and price the runtime-check variants inside the spike.

Design and adversarially review the trust boundary for the leading composition: how FFI/host/I-O buffers acquire facts on entry, views/slices semantics that keep aliasing explicit while preserving vectorization legality (A001 negative scope), and whether an explicitly-unsafe tier is compatible with the soundness red line.

Conduct a workload census of the language's intended program population: fraction fitting the regular/affine fragment, prevalence of dynamic/symbolic shapes, and sparse/irregular requirements — this directly resolves whether privileging the structured core optimizes the right center.

Define the FP reduction ordering/reassociation policy before fixing any parallel-loop or reduction-construct semantics, since A005 makes math flags a legality input gating all three positions equally.

Draft the composed architecture as the explicit null hypothesis for the next debate round: contract-bearing substrate + structured default surface + schedule layer for the pure fragment, and reframe the question as 'choose the default surface, checking-burden location, and failure modes' rather than an exclusive winner.
Bring the N/D/C/J card series into the read set and re-examine this topic if numerics, concurrency, or JIT cards bear on reduction semantics, layout, or dynamic shapes.

Specify the language's guarantee contract: which facts are guaranteed surfaced/preserved/consumed versus which transformations are best-effort (A005, A010), so performance-transparency promises are scoped to what any backend can honor.

question: What dispatch and generics model should the performance core use?

positionAssessments.position: Advocate 1: Monomorphization-only core with an explicit, restricted, visibly-costed dyn construct
positionAssessments.strengths: Best direct card support of the three: D001 (monomorphization yields concrete-code runtime behavior) is the corpus's most direct generics card; D002's positive scope explicitly supports making dynamic dispatch explicit when used; D003 attests the restricted-dyn escape hatch is precedented. Its central asymmetry — source-level guarantee vs conditional recovery — is genuinely in the corpus: D007's negative scope (speculative devirt may be reverted, option-dependent), D008 (interposition blocks IPA), F007 and misleading-comparisons note 3 (managed runtimes recover performance conditionally, unlike source-level guarantees). Best fit to the charter (ergonomics sacrificeable, transparency prioritized). Concessions were honest and pre-empted several critic attacks. Notably, all three advocates converge on guaranteed specialization for representation-affecting (value-type/hot) paths — A1 is the common skeleton.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: The critic landed four real hits. (1) F009 is cited backwards: the corpus's only workflow-confirmed dispatch-adjacent card exemplifies Swift SIL, a shared-generics/witness-table IR that preserves generic semantics so specialization can be decided in the optimizer — it evidences a rival architecture at least as much as A1's frontend-monomorphization framing (though it is compatible with a semantic specialization guarantee implemented at IR level). (2) The load-bearing claim that erased/shared bodies 'cannot carry type-dependent alias facts' is uncarded inference, contestable (uniform pointer-shaped bodies can carry parameter-level noalias), and exactly the move misleading-comparisons note 5 polices. (3) Predictability double standard: A1's own D004 shows monomorphized code's optimization is codegen-unit/LTO-option-dependent, so the position silently needs a build/visibility model (i.e., part of A2). (4) Composition hole: per D003, dyn-compatible traits exclude generic dispatchable methods, so mono-only plus strict dyn offers no sanctioned path for heterogeneous generic APIs — A1's own risk list predicts hand-rolled void*/fn-pointer erasure that the optimizer and F003 machinery handle worse, with no mitigation offered. Minor overreaches: D008 'sidestep' claim, Julia 'only/manually' additions to D009. Core cost (code size/compile time) is flagged by D001/D009 negative scopes and quantified nowhere.

positionAssessments.position: Advocate 2: Closed-world whole-program compilation (sealed-by-default, mandatory LTO-style visibility, no interposition/reflection/dynamic loading in core)
positionAssessments.strengths: Correctly identifies that visibility and finality gate the documented devirtualization machinery (D004, D007), and D008 gives genuine, specific support for no-interposition module semantics (converting an unverified linking assumption into a language invariant). D005's negative scope really does support restricting reflection in a performance core. The position composes with either generics model and several of its elements (no-interposition modules, finality semantics feeding D007-class machinery) survive as valuable amendments regardless of the generics decision.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: The critic's strongest attack lands squarely: 'guaranteed / sound-by-construction / invariant' devirtualization is supported by no card — D007 documents only conditional, revertible, option-dependent mechanisms, and LLVM's actual WPD machinery is uncovered (A2's own gap list). Closed world makes devirtualization possible without guards; nothing in evidence makes it promised, and inliner heuristics remain heuristics in a closed world. The universal claim 'every documented obstacle is an open-world escape hatch' is refuted in-corpus (D004 codegen-unit boundaries are build artifacts; D009's Function-typed fields hide call targets inside closed programs — and no position handles closure/function-value dispatch). Structurally, this is a visibility/build model, not an answer to the dispatch-and-generics question — it borrows A1's monomorphization (D001) for the actual topic, so it is an amendment presented as a rival. Under the soundness rule, core/periphery fact attenuation across the FFI/dynamic-linking boundary (D008; the interop dossier's targets include dynamic linking, JVM/.NET, Python) is a precondition, not a risk, and it is undesigned. Ban-vs-restrict (reflection) and 'unverifiable' (interposition flag) exceed what the cards say.

positionAssessments.position: Advocate 3: Hybrid .NET-direction generics — specialize value-type instantiations, share one body across reference-type instantiations, static specialize/share boundary, devirtualization backstop
positionAssessments.strengths: The only position that takes the corpus's carded specialization costs seriously (D001 and D009 negative scopes: code size, compile time, cache pressure from over-specialization). D005 attests the hybrid is a shipped, load-bearing design, not a paper proposal — the only card documenting a bounded-code-growth generics strategy. The static, type-system-visible specialize/share boundary is a genuine transparency property versus JIT-heuristic specialization cliffs (D009, D010). Concessions were unusually candid (JIT-vs-AOT transfer, undecided reference-type representation).
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Three critic attacks land hard. (1) 'Pure code-size win with no representational compromise' is contradicted by its own risk #1, by D002's cost class on the shared path, and by uncarded .NET dictionary/type-handle lookup costs — what is lost is the compiler's knowledge of type-argument-dependent call targets. (2) The D007 backstop is mis-transferred (GCC C++ vtable devirtualization, not shared-generic/dictionary dispatch) and circular where it would work: recovery-by-specializing-hot-instantiations regenerates exactly the code growth the hybrid promises to bound. (3) It never prices the shared path against the corpus's strongest-attested evidence class — whether shared bodies can discharge F003 noalias/alias.scope obligations — a potential tax on the flagship optimization exactly where the design shares. Feasibility rests on two undecided priors: a uniform reference-type representation (which the performance core may not have) and an AOT instantiation-closure story (D005's precedent is a JIT with lazy instantiation; NativeAOT-style evidence is absent). 'Only design attested to deliver both' overstates D005, whose negative scope disclaims proof of JIT optimization quality.

keyTradeoffs: Guaranteed specialization (A1) vs bounded code growth (A3): the single trade every generics design must make; the corpus contains zero quantitative cards on runtime, code size, i-cache, or compile time (caveats.md), so this axis is empirically unarbitrable today — a decision now would be a charter-values commitment, not an evidence-driven one.

Guarantee-by-construction vs recovery-by-optimization: D001-style source guarantees vs D007/D008/F007-style conditional, revertible, option-dependent recovery. The corpus qualitatively favors the guarantee side for a transparency-first language, but never measures how much performance speculative/PGO devirtualization actually leaves on the table.

Meaning of 'performance transparency': predictable cost model vs guaranteed-optimization floor (e.g., may the compiler silently devirtualize an explicit dyn call it proves unique?). No card addresses this; A1 raises it as a gap while A2 and A3 silently assume opposite answers. This charter question is antecedent to the topic decision.

Closed-world visibility (devirtualization possible without guards, interposition banned per D008) vs build scalability, dynamic-loading capability, and FFI-boundary soundness (D004/D007 compile-time/memory costs; dossier interop targets require a periphery whose fact-attenuation is undesigned).

Escape-hatch expressiveness vs analyzability: strict dyn-compatibility (D003) keeps runtime representation coherent but excludes generic dispatchable methods, leaving heterogeneous generic workloads with no sanctioned path — risking user-rolled erasure the optimizer understands worse than a looser language construct would be.

Where the specialization decision lives: frontend monomorphization (A1) vs IR-level generics with optimizer-decided or annotation-guaranteed specialization (the F009/Swift-SIL fourth model no advocate proposed) — affects separate compilation, generic ABI, and code-size control, and is entirely unexamined.
evidenceGaps: No quantitative comparison anywhere in the read corpus of monomorphization vs shared-body vs dictionary-passing generics on runtime, code size, i-cache behavior, or compile time — the decisive measurement for the A1-vs-A3 axis (caveats.md: documentation evidence does not quantify magnitude, size, or compile-time cost).

LLVM whole-program devirtualization machinery (!type metadata, -fwhole-program-vtables, virtual-function elimination) has no card; for an LLVM-targeted language this is the most load-bearing implementation question behind A2's claims and A1/A3's backstops. D007 is GCC documentation only.

Swift witness-table/@_specialize/resilience-domain generics — the strongest unrepresented model (separately-compiled generics with opt-in guaranteed specialization), gestured at by F009 (the only workflow-confirmed dispatch-adjacent card) and listed as Open in the dossier.

The constructive question that arbitrates A1's core inference vs A3's core mechanism: can a shared/erased generic body soundly discharge F003 noalias/alias.scope obligations, and at what precision loss vs per-instantiation code? Checkable against LLVM semantics without benchmarks; no one has done it.

AOT realization of the hybrid: how .NET NativeAOT / Mono full-AOT compute value-type instantiation closure and when they fall back to universal shared code — determines whether D005 transfers to an AOT LLVM target at all.
All load-bearing dispatch cards (D001, D002, D003, D005, D007) are provisional direct extractions, not adversarially verified (caveats.md standing rule).

The N001-N006, A001-A010, C001-C006, and J001-J005 card series named in the debate ground rules do not appear in any of the five assigned files; all three advocates and the critic flagged this — the debate effectively ran on the D/F subset only.

Core/periphery fact attenuation: how optimizer-visible facts are soundly weakened across FFI/dynamic-linking boundaries (D008 class) is undesigned; under the soundness-inviolable rule this is a precondition for any closed-world or no-interposition commitment.

Dispatch through first-class functions and closures is unaddressed by every position, despite D009 documenting Function-typed fields hiding call targets as an optimizer defeat inside fully closed programs.

Relative importance of dispatch strategy vs layout, numerics, and concurrency for overall performance is not established — the corpus is aliasing-heavy by selection (caveats.md), so the stakes of this topic are themselves unmeasured.

Cross-unit instantiation strategies (rustc share-generics, instantiation deduplication) needed to bound the mono-only code-size and compile-time risk (D004 shows unit/LTO boundaries already stress the model).
leadingCandidate: Advocate 1's skeleton — guaranteed static specialization as the core's only implicit path, with dynamic dispatch available solely through an explicit, restricted (dyn-compatible, D003-style) construct whose stated cost model does not rely on devirtualization — carried forward with A2's no-interposition/finality module semantics as a compatible amendment, and with the 'monomorphization-ONLY' foreclosure of D005-style bounded sharing explicitly held open pending measurement.
rationale: Three considerations drive the ranking. First, all three advocates converge on guaranteed static specialization wherever representation differs per instantiation (A2 borrows D001 outright; A3 specializes all value types), so the genuinely contested residue is narrow: whether reference-path instantiations may share bodies, and what build/visibility model surrounds the core. A1's skeleton is that consensus core, and it has the most direct card support in the corpus (D001, D002, D003) plus the best-attested qualitative asymmetry: source-level guarantees vs conditional, revertible recovery (D007 negative scope, D008, F007, misleading-comparisons note 3) — an asymmetry that matters specifically because the charter makes performance transparency the goal and lets ergonomics pay for it. Second, the rivals took more structural damage from the critic: A2's central 'guaranteed devirtualization' claim has no card behind it (LLVM WPD is uncovered) and the position is a visibility amendment rather than an answer to the topic; A3's mechanism rests on two undecided priors (uniform reference representation, AOT instantiation closure), its D007 backstop is mis-transferred from C++ vtables and circular where it works, and it never priced its shared path against the F003 alias-obligation question. A1's damage, by contrast, was to auxiliary inferences (the inverted F009 citation, the uncarded alias-discharge claim, predictability absolutism) and to a repairable composition hole — its core mechanism was not undermined. Third, per ground rule 4 this cannot be settled now: the decisive specialization-vs-code-growth trade has zero quantitative evidence, every load-bearing D-card is provisional, the strongest workflow-confirmed card (F009) mildly points at an unexamined Swift-style fourth model, and four whole card series named in the ground rules were absent from the assigned files. Hence design_candidate_later rather than a decision: A1's skeleton leads clearly on in-hand evidence and charter fit, but it hardens into a design only if the verification program below fails to overturn it — in particular, if the F003-discharge check vindicates the claim that sharing erodes alias facts, and if code-size/compile-time measurements do not show mono-only losing in size-bound regimes. If shared bodies prove able to discharge alias obligations cheaply and bloat proves severe, the leader should shift toward a bounded-sharing or Swift-style annotated-specialization hybrid.
followUps: Adversarially verify the load-bearing provisional cards D001, D002, D003, D005, D007 (per the caveats.md standing rule) before any further weight is placed on them.

Run the construction-level check the critic identified as decisive: determine against LLVM semantics whether a shared/erased generic body can soundly discharge F003 noalias/alias.scope obligations, and quantify the precision loss vs per-instantiation code; record the result as a new evidence card.

Build a dossier on LLVM whole-program devirtualization (!type metadata, -fwhole-program-vtables, virtual-function elimination) with version-dated sources — the missing mechanism behind both A2's guarantees and A1/A3's backstops.

Build a dossier on Swift generics ABI (witness tables, @_specialize, resilience domains) and formally evaluate the F009-implied fourth model: IR-level generics with guaranteed-specialization annotations, combining A1's guarantees on marked paths with bounded sharing elsewhere.

Define and execute a measurement program with pre-committed decision thresholds: monomorphization vs shared-body vs dictionary-passing on representative workloads, measuring runtime, code size, i-cache behavior, and compile time; include rustc share-generics/deduplication data to bound the mono-only risk.
Gather evidence on .NET NativeAOT and Mono full-AOT instantiation-closure strategies and universal-shared fallbacks, to establish whether the D005 hybrid transfers to an AOT LLVM target.

Design spike on escape-hatch composition: generic methods on dyn interfaces (excluded by D003), instantiation at dyn/erased types, and heterogeneous generic registries — either close A1's composition hole or document its cost as a known limitation.

Resolve the antecedent charter question: does performance transparency mean a predictable cost model or a guaranteed-optimization floor — specifically, whether the compiler may silently devirtualize an explicit dyn call it can prove unique.

Locate and surface the N001-N006, A001-A010, C001-C006, and J001-J005 card series referenced in the ground rules; re-weigh this debate if they contain dispatch-relevant evidence (plausibly C++, numerics, and JIT material).

Design the core/periphery fact-attenuation mechanism for FFI and dynamic-linking boundaries (D008 class) before adopting any no-interposition or closed-world module semantics — a soundness precondition, not an optimization detail.

Add evidence and a design position on dispatch through first-class functions and closures (D009's Function-typed-field defeat), which no current position covers despite affecting iterators, callbacks, and async in every candidate design.

question: Should any optimizer-relevant facts be left to profile-driven or speculative runtime machinery instead of source contracts?

positionAssessments.position: Fully static AOT: every optimizer-relevant fact is a verified source contract; no guards, no deopt, no warmup
positionAssessments.strengths: Occupies all of the adversarially confirmed territory: F001/F002 (non-interference contracts enable register promotion, load/store elimination, reordering in shipping AOT languages), F003/F004 (LLVM carriers exist), F005/F006 (Fortran rule-assumed model; SPARK statically-verified route), F009 (semantics-preserving IR). It is the only position whose mechanism is proven in production AOT compilers, and its demand that contracts be verified (not asserted) correctly answers the F003 UB soundness condition. The qualitative costs of the rival runtime channel are documented, if provisionally (J002 invalidation machinery, J005 warmup/code-cache, J004/F007 conditionality).
positionAssessments.weaknesses: The critic's attacks largely land. The universal quantifier forfeits frequency facts (hotness, branch bias, receiver distributions) that have no known sound contract encoding — conceded in its own gaps — and forfeits J002's (provisional) class of profile-enabled optimizations by fiat, with unbounded unquantified cost. Several headline claims are overclaimed against the cards: 'guards paid by every execution forever' contradicts J002's invalidation-based design; 'zero runtime checks' is not what F006 says about SPARK; 'Fortran proves production viability' exceeds documentation-grade evidence per caveats.md; 'pipeline complete end-to-end' restates misleading-comparisons entry 5's flagged claim. The F007/J004 conditionality argument never shows a static contract guaranteeing allocation elimination — conditionality may be inherent to escape analysis. Its own strongest cards' negative scopes (F001 unsafe/restrict truthfulness, F005 POINTER/TARGET/coarrays) evidence that every studied static channel needed escape hatches, i.e., pure static coverage is incomplete. AOT's own costs (monomorphization code size, compile time, annotation/verification burden) are left off the ledger, exactly the dimensions caveats.md says are unquantified.

positionAssessments.position: Static correctness contracts + firewalled offline profile data for cost decisions only (guarded fallback, no deopt)
positionAssessments.strengths: Its core partition principle is the one discriminating argument actually derivable from confirmed cards: F003 makes noalias-family facts UB-on-violation, so a measurement of past runs can never soundly carry them — wrong contract = miscompile, wrong profile-steered cost decision = slowdown. This failure-mode-matched channel assignment survives the critic intact and fits the soundness-first charter. It fully captures the consensus territory (F001/F002/F004/F006), avoids the documented runtime-machinery costs (J002 deopt/invalidation, J005 warmup/code-cache), keeps optimization inputs inspectable build artifacts (consistent with F009's visibility lesson), and partially retains J002's conceded profile-enabled benefits via guarded fast paths with compiled statically-correct fallbacks.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: The PGO half rests on zero corpus cards — its own gap 1, confirmed by my reading: no offline-PGO evidence exists in any assigned file. The J003 citation ('concrete instance of the firewall working' via field ordering/hot-cold splitting) is fabricated; the card contains only V8 hidden-class content. 'Captures exactly that benefit' is contradicted by its own concession: non-local speculatable facts (field-never-written, map-never-transitions) need invalidation-style machinery the firewall forbids, so the capture is partial to an unmeasured degree. The firewall leaks by its own wording (guarded correctness speculation such as alias versioning is admitted, and that boundary is undefined precisely where it matters), and 'wrong profile ⇒ slower, never wrong' is an unverified implementation property — no card shows LLVM profile inputs are correctness-isolated. Profiles as build inputs also weaken strict source-determines-performance transparency and add reproducibility burden.

positionAssessments.position: Selective speculation runtime: contracts by default, guarded specialization with deoptimization at explicitly designated boundaries
positionAssessments.strengths: Correctly identifies, from confirmed cards' negative scopes (F001 unsafe/restrict, F005 POINTER/TARGET, F006 usability costs), that a residual set of true-but-statically-unprovable facts is normal, not exotic. Guard-plus-fallback is sound by construction where assert-and-eat-UB is not, honoring the non-negotiable soundness rule. It is the only position that can monetize non-local invalidation-style facts (per J002's provisional negative scope), and its source-visible speculation marks are a genuine attempt to reconcile speculation with performance transparency (F009 analogy). Its concessions are the most honest in the debate about the provisional status of its own evidence.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: The critic's false-trichotomy attack lands on the position's defining component: guarded fast path with a compiled statically-correct fallback (no deopt) is a fourth option, so 'only guard-plus-deopt is fast and sound' is false, and deoptimization — the thing distinguishing this position from position 2 — is supported by zero AOT evidence (own gap 1). All supporting cards are provisional J-cards from whole-program JITs for dynamic/managed, effectively single-mutator languages; concurrency (unverified corpus territory per caveats.md) undermines cheap guard-then-use. Guard-class mismatch: J001-J003 evidence cheap tag/shape checks, but the residual boundaries it targets need alias guards, unevidenced here — and that is where the F002 payoff lives. Deopt machinery is a language-wide tax even when rarely used (own concession 7), with F008 (funclet exit paths forbidding register allocation for live-across values, design-generalization confidence low) the only — and uncited — corpus analogue for how exit-path machinery pessimizes surrounding code. J002's one-directional de-specialization reintroduces input-history-dependent performance opacity, in direct tension with the transparency charter unless cliff diagnostics become mandatory language features.

keyTradeoffs: Failure-mode asymmetry per fact class: a false contract lowered to noalias-family IR is a miscompile (F003 UB), a stale profile-steered cost decision is a slowdown (if profile inputs are truly correctness-isolated — unverified), and a failed guard is a cliff or deopt churn (J002 one-directional degradation). The channel for each fact class should match its tolerable failure mode; only the first leg of this is confirmed evidence.

Optimization capture vs predictability: J002's provisional negative scope says deopt-supported speculation enables optimizations static compilers cannot legally do without profiles; forfeiting them (position 1) is an unbounded unquantified sacrifice, while keeping them (position 3) buys history-dependent performance opacity and invalidation machinery — and no card measures either side's magnitude.

Contract-channel costs vs runtime-channel costs: SPARK-style verification burden and general-language usability (F006 negative scope), annotation density, monomorphization code size, and compile time are unquantified, exactly like the rival's warmup, code-cache, and guard costs (J005, J001 negative scope); the corpus explicitly refuses magnitude rankings (caveats.md, misleading-comparisons entries 1 and 3).

Per-site guard cost vs language-wide machinery tax: compiled-in guards (position 2) cost every fast-path execution but keep the pipeline simple; deopt support (position 3) can remove fast-path checks via invalidation but taxes the whole compiler with frame-reconstruction metadata and optimization barriers even when speculation is rare — F008 is the only (weak, low-confidence) corpus analogue for that pessimization, and the 2-vs-3 delta is priced by no card.

Transparency granularity: position 1 offers source-determines-machine-code; position 2 offers source-plus-inspectable-profile-artifact; position 3 offers source-visible speculation sites whose realized performance state is input-history-dependent. The corpus contains no card measuring the value of any of these predictability levels (startup, tail latency, real-time).
evidenceGaps: No offline/AOT PGO cards at all (LLVM instrumentation PGO, AutoFDO/sample PGO, BOLT/Propeller): magnitude of inlining/layout/devirt wins, staleness sensitivity, regression rates. This is the single most decisive gap — it adjudicates position 1 vs 2 and prices position 2's entire PGO half.

No AOT-plus-deopt evidence (LLVM deoptimization operand bundles/statepoints, GraalVM native image, .NET tiered compilation with dynamic PGO): needed to price position 3's defining mechanism and the position 2-vs-3 delta (compiled fallback vs deopt), which no current card touches.

Residual guard cost vs static knowledge is unquantified — explicitly named in J001's negative scope — including whether guards hoist/combine well under LLVM and what alias-guard (loop-versioning) checks cost where the F002-class payoff lives.

All J-cards (J001-J005) are provisional direct extractions, not adversarially verified; the pivot sentence of the whole debate (J002 negative scope: deopt enables optimizations static compilers cannot legally do without profiles) needs verification before any decision leans on it.

The N/A/D/C card series referenced in the debate brief do not exist in the readable corpus; the D-series (dispatch) bears directly on speculative devirtualization, every position's flagship example, so all dispatch reasoning here is mechanism-plausible but corpus-unevidenced.

No evidence that LLVM profile inputs (branch weights, value profiles) are correctness-isolated — i.e., that no pass converts profile metadata into semantic assumptions; position 2's firewall is unenforceable-as-evidenced without this.

No quantification of the static channel's own costs: contract-authoring density, verification time at scale (F006 negative scope), monomorphization code-size growth, compile time — caveats.md lists these as exactly what documentation evidence does not show.

No measurement of the residual set size: how often contracts are genuinely impractical (FFI, plugins, deserialized shapes, open-world dispatch) in optimizer-first codebases; if near-empty, positions 2 and 3's machinery is unjustified, if large, position 1's forfeiture is severe.

Concurrency and memory models are unverified corpus territory (caveats.md): guard-then-use atomicity outside single-mutator runtimes, safepoint costs, and noalias-contract interaction with a concurrent memory model are undiscussed by all positions.

FFI/external-call boundaries: no card covers how contract facts survive extern calls, how guards could check foreign effects, or how any of the three designs keeps F003's soundness condition across them — the most common real boundary, unaddressed by everyone.

No card measures predictability's value directly (variance, tail latency, startup-sensitive or real-time deployments); position 1's headline pillar and position 2's transparency compromise are both unquantified.

The critic's proposed third channel — profile-checked source annotations (frequency hints validated against profiles at build time, error on contradiction) — is entirely unexplored; it could dissolve the frequency-facts-cannot-be-contracts argument and deserves a card of its own.
leadingCandidate: Static correctness contracts as the default channel, plus firewalled offline profile data restricted to cost decisions (position 2) — leading on the strength of its confirmed-evidence core (F003's UB-on-violation argument that correctness-grade facts can never be profile-sourced, and F001/F002/F006's confirmed contract channel), with its PGO half, firewall enforceability, and the guarded-fallback-vs-deopt boundary against position 3 explicitly unresolved pending evidence.
rationale: The adversarially confirmed cards (F001-F006, F009) settle only what all three positions already agree on: statically checked non-interference contracts, lowered to LLVM's UB-grade carriers only when the frontend obligation is verified, are the best-attested and default channel — and per misleading-comparisons entry 5 those carriers are conditional, not a solved pipeline. Every claim that discriminates among the positions lives where the corpus has thin or no evidence: the J-cards are provisional (ground rule 3), the debate's pivot sentence (J002's negative scope) is unverified, and there are zero cards on offline PGO, AOT-plus-deopt, guard-cost magnitude, dispatch (the D-series is unreadable), concurrency, or the size of the residual set of statically unprovable facts. The critic's verification-grade attacks confirmed this: position 1's absolutist headline claims overrun its cards (SPARK zero-runtime-checks, Fortran production viability, forever-tax, pipeline-complete), position 2's PGO half has no cards and one fabricated citation (J003), and position 3's defining mechanism — deoptimization — is supported by no AOT evidence and rests on a false trichotomy. Position 2 leads because its core partition principle is the only discriminating argument derivable from confirmed evidence: F003 makes correctness-grade facts UB-on-violation, so they cannot soundly come from measurements of past runs, while cost decisions fail only into slowdowns — a failure-mode-matched channel assignment that fits the soundness-first charter. But 'leading' cannot become 'clearly leading pending verification': the positions 2 and 3 nearly collapse into one another (both are contracts-plus-guarded-speculation; they differ on offline-vs-runtime profiles and compiled-fallback-vs-deopt) and no card prices that delta; the firewall's enforceability in LLVM is unverified; and caveats.md's standing rule forbids design decisions that outrun the cards.
